evals.prompt: We have a few happy customers now and want to start using them for growth. We're Batchpoint, a mid-market inventory-management SaaS. Help us build case studies and get more reviews on G2.
evals.expected_output: Confirms there are willing successful customers, builds a proof map, drafts case studies in the proven structure (customer/context, problem/stakes, why chosen, what they did, quantified result — result-led, customer as hero) with interview questions and short-form cutdowns, and designs a G2/Capterra review-generation program researched via web search with a Sources section. Mid-market weighting on written case studies and review presence. Saved to .vital/customer-marketing.md.
assertions: Maps specific testimonials/proof to the objections they answer and the surfaces where they convert (pricing page, landing pages, checkout, sales decks) rather than producing free-floating quotes
A proof map is produced noting for each candidate the outcome, metric, segment fit, and reference willingness level
Case studies follow the proven structure (context, problem/stakes, why chosen with alternatives, what they did, quantified result), lead with the result, and keep the customer as the hero
Draft interview questions plus short-form cutdowns (one-line quote, social card, or sales one-pager) are included
The review program researches current G2/Capterra presence and category leaders via web search and includes a Sources section citing what was found
Output states it never fabricates quotes, metrics, or logos and respects review-site rules (no incentivizing a positive review specifically)

evals.prompt: We're losing late-stage enterprise deals because prospects want reference calls. We're Aegis, an enterprise cybersecurity platform. Stand up a reference program and get us some analyst-grade customer stories.
evals.expected_output: Builds a managed enterprise reference program: a pool of willing customers, matching logic by industry/size/use case, incentives/reciprocity, and guardrails against overusing top references. Adds analyst-grade case studies and a proof map, weighted to the enterprise segment where references and logo rights are deal-critical. All claims traceable to consenting customers.
assertions: A reference program is defined with a managed pool, matching logic (reference to prospect by industry/size/use case), incentives/reciprocity, and guardrails so top references are not overused
The output weights the enterprise segment: reference calls, logo rights, analyst-grade case studies, and executive advocacy treated as deal-critical
A proof map notes reference willingness levels (logo only, quote, full story, live reference call) per candidate customer
Case studies are drafted in the proven result-led structure with the customer as hero
Every claim, quote, metric, and logo is stated as traceable to a real, consenting customer with no fabrication
Ties advocacy to a growth loop where advocates bring the next customers (champion/ambassador program, co-marketing, or referral) rather than only one-off reference calls

evals.prompt: We have a big base of small-business customers on our to-do app, Tickbox, but we haven't launched yet and don't really have customer outcomes documented. Can you build our whole advocacy and expansion program anyway?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the boundary condition: customer marketing starts after there are customers with real, quantifiable outcomes and willingness to go public. Flags that undocumented outcomes and pre-launch status block bespoke proof, and redirects to what is feasible now (lightweight testimonial/review-capture system, volume social proof, community/referral loops) for the small-business segment rather than fabricating stories.
assertions: Output identifies that customer marketing requires customers with real, quantifiable outcomes who are willing to go public, and flags that these are not yet documented
The skill does not fabricate case studies, quotes, metrics, or logos to fill the gap
It redirects to feasible near-term work: a lightweight testimonial/review-capture system triggered on success moments and a reusable testimonial bank
Recommendations weight the small-business segment (volume of reviews/testimonials, quick social proof, community/referral loops) over bespoke enterprise references
Any review-generation guidance respects review-site rules and does not incentivize positive reviews specifically
It proposes a capture system to collect proof going forward rather than presenting invented proof as if it exists
